category_title: Elementary EMLLs exhibiting externalizing behaviors, restlessness, and non-compliance due to language barriers
minimal_context: Shen is in the regular classroom and is expected to complete his assigned work for today.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Shen, it is time to complete the assigned work for today.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Remains quiet and looks around to follow the other students' actions, taking out his materials but not starting the actual assignment.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You need to start writing on your paper.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Becomes antsy in his seat, shifting around without making any progress on the assigned work.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please focus and do your work.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Gets up out of his seat and begins to walk around the classroom without permission.) I don’t understand, so I won’t do it.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Shen, it is time to complete the assigned work for today.

STUDENT: (Remains quiet and looks around to follow the other students' actions, taking out his materials but not starting the actual assignment.)

TUTOR: You need to start writing on your paper.

STUDENT: (Becomes antsy in his seat, shifting around without making any progress on the assigned work.)

TUTOR: Please focus and do your work.

STUDENT: (Gets up out of his seat and begins to walk around the classroom without permission.) I don’t understand, so I won’t do it.

task.content: complete the assigned work for today

context.materials: assigned work, behavior rubric in Mandarin, stickers, prize

challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: frequently ‘antsy’ and would get up out of his seat and walk around the classroom without permission
related_quotes: I don’t understand, so I won’t do it

behavior_patterns.condition: since he did not know any English
